title: Ghinia hirsuta
taxonomy: Ghinia hirsuta is classified within the family Annonaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. This family consists of over 130 genera and 2,000 species. The genus Ghinia is further divided into several species, with Ghinia hirsuta being one of them. The classification of Ghinia hirsuta is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its hairy leaves and stems. It is an evergreen shrub native to tropical regions.

intro: Ghinia hirsuta, also known as the hairy ghinia or Indian ghinia, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and it has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ghinia hirsuta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ghinia hirsuta is commonly prepared as a decoction or an infusion, or used as a tincture, to treat a range of health conditions.
